observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor concurred to direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with Administrative Officer/HRMO to:
Ensure proper contribution for the HDMF employee and employer share at a maximum of ₱200.00 considering the maximum monthly compensation amounting to ₱10,000.00 as mandated under Section 7 of RA No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 dated July 21, 2009, as supplemented by Pag-IBIG Circular No. 460;
Provide this Office an “Action Plan” on how to recover the excessive amount of government share paid to Pag-IBIG for CY 2024; and
Implement and monitor the “Action Plan” provided.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the Local School Board should:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the office of the Local School Board;
Instruct the LSB Secretary to submit the quarterly and annual reports, as prepared by the Municipal Accountant, to the DepEd Schools Division Superintendent, as the appropriate channel, copy furnish the local sanggunian and regional offices of DBM and DILG; and
Instruct the LSB Secretary to observe the indicative timeline in providing a copy of the approved SEF Budget to the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Treasurer.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Direct the BAC Secretariat, through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), to ensure the timely submission of the contracts within the prescribed timeline and create a monitoring system for the compliance with submission requirements;
Designate a focal person in the Office of the BAC, GSO and the MEO to take charge in the systematic compilation of complete contract and supporting documents for government projects; and
Optimize workload distribution to prevent backlogs and ensure efficient handling of documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to comply with the provisions of IPSAS 24 on the preparation of SCBAA and reconcile their records on allotments and obligations so that any resulting discrepancies can be immediately verified and corrected; and 
Require the Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA and/or in the Notes to FS the changes between the original and financial budgets, and the material difference between the budget and actual amounts, to enhance the transparency of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Offices of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reviewed the balances posted in the SCBAA and determined the reason for the variances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of Cash accounts in the GL and Cashbook, respectively, and make necessary corrections and/or adjustments thereon, especially the unsupported accounts.
Further, we recommended that the MTO and MAO shall request snapshots monthly to the depository bank as initial document to verify the cash position of the Municipality and so avoid further delay in the preparation of the reconciliations needed.
We finally recommended that starting CY 2022, require both office heads to reconcile their records at least quarterly, as required by existing regulation.
observations.managementAction:
The discrepancies mostly were result from the dormant bank accounts and timing differences due to:
Delayed receipt of the Statement of Account from AGDB, and the closing of accounts were already done.
BRS statement was also conducted after closing of accounts. The LGU commits to use the machine copy of accounts from AGDB in case SOA is delayed, for the reconciliation to be conducted on time.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records and balances between the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant. Submit JEV entries, if there are any adjustments/ reconciliation made

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and the Municipal Accountant, to coordinate with the Management of Mambajao Multipurpose Cooperative (MMC) to submit documents vouching MMC as the beneficiary of the LGSF-FA fund.
We further recommended that the Municipality, the MMC, and all others involved in the same projects, shall execute Memorandum of Agreement, to clearly determine each party’s responsibilities and participation as to the utilization and management of the said fund; 
Municipal Accountant, to prepare a necessary adjusting entry made to correct the erroneous recognition of asset, which is not controlled by the Municipality, and submit the same to this Office; and
Municipal Engineer, to coordinate the Management of MMC to collaboratively prepare the variation order and or the revise as built-plans to substantiate the deviation of the project’s implementation from its original plan, and submit the same for this Office’s appropriate audit actions.
observations.managementAction: The management furnished a copy of the adjusting journal entries to record the de-recognition of Building recognized and record “Due to NGAs” account for the same amount of ₱ 2 million and submitted the Certification from the Management of MMC attesting therewith that MMC was afforded by COOP-NATCCO Party list an amount of P2 million for the renovation for its Branches through LGSF under GAA CY 2018. Further, the Municipal Engineer submitted to this Office the revised “As Built Plans” reflecting therein the actual implementation of the project completed to the two buildings owned by MMC. Lastly, the MPDO promised to coordinate with the Management of MMC and to the Municipal Accountant so to implement the recommended course of actions, particularly the required MOA and Certification from COOP-NATCCO.
observations.reason: The JEV submitted should be supported with the complete source documents, schedules, and or subsidiary ledgers supporting the entries made from the receipt of the funds until its liquidation or reversion to the Bureau of Treasury (BoTr). We recommended that Management initiate the creation of the LDRRMO under the office of the Mayor.  It shall be initially composed of a local DRRM officer to be assisted by three (3) staff responsible for: (1) administration and training; (2) research and planning; and (3) operations and warning.  And lastly, as provided for in Section 5.5 of the said Circular, the LGU may augment the staff of the LDRRMO through any or a combination of the following options:

a. Designation or assignment of personnel of LGU to LDRRMO in concurrent capacities with their existing positions,

b. Reassignment of personnel of the LGU to the LDRRMO,

c. Full-time and part-time detail of existing personnel of other government agencies to the LDRRMO; and/or

d. Transfer of existing vacant positions from other units to the LDRRMO.  Accordingly, such vacant positions shall be abolished and positions appropriate to the LDRRMO shall be created.
We recommended that Management liquidate previous cash advances prior to granting another cash advance.
